class Solution:
    def isMatch(self, s: str, p: str) -> bool:
        m, n = len(s), len(p)

        # dp[i][j] will be true if the first i characters of s match the first j characters of p
        dp = [[False] * (n + 1) for _ in range(m + 1)]

        # Base case: empty string matches empty pattern
        dp[0][0] = True

        # Fill the first row (s is empty)
        # Only '*' can match an empty string
        for j in range(1, n + 1):
            if p[j-1] == '*':
                dp[0][j] = dp[0][j-1]
            # else: dp[0][j] remains False (empty string cannot match a non-empty non-star pattern)

        # Fill the rest of the DP table
        for i in range(1, m + 1):
            for j in range(1, n + 1):
                if p[j-1] == s[i-1] or p[j-1] == '?':
                    # Current characters match or '?' matches s[i-1]
                    # Rely on the match of the preceding substrings
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j-1]
                elif p[j-1] == '*':
                    # '*' can match an empty sequence (dp[i][j-1])
                    # OR '*' can match one or more characters (dp[i-1][j])
                    dp[i][j] = dp[i][j-1] or dp[i-1][j]
                # else: p[j-1] is a regular character that does not match s[i-1]
                # In this case, dp[i][j] remains False (its initialized default value)
        
        return dp[m][n]
